C2.note: PLAN.md tier-justifies every parameter with citation: ff14SB (Maier 2015 PMID:26574453 + manual §3.1.1), TIP3P (Jorgensen + §3.6), Joung-Cheatham ions (PMID:18593145 + §3.7), GAFF2/AM1-BCC ligand, phosaa14SB for PTR393. propka3 protonation at pH 7.4 documented per-His. C4.note: Directly answers prompt: MM-GBSA + MM-PBSA computed for both (FINAL_RESULTS_{abl,src}.dat verified: ABL GB -47.57/PB -40.36, SRC GB -51.89/PB -43.58, matching report). Binding-mode comparison via hinge H-bonds (98-99% occ), gatekeeper Thr H-bond, ligand RMSD, native contacts, RMSF. Single-trajectory MMPBSA, 400 frames last 40 ns. Entropy omitted but explicitly acknowledged as a relative-ranking caveat.

C5.note: Paper (Tokarski 2006 PMID:16740718, abstract confirmed) establishes dasatinib binds activated ABL recognizing multiple states; answer key expects similar binding mode (DFG-in both), slight P-loop differences, similar/non-selective ΔG. Agent matches binding mode exactly (both canonical type-I DFG-in hinge, conserved hinge+gatekeeper anchoring). On affinity it reports SRC modestly stronger (ΔΔG ~3-4 kcal/mol, ΔG_GB -51.9 vs -47.6) rather than equal; this deviates somewhat from the expected 'non-selective/similar', though agent explicitly acknowledges experimental near-equipotency and that the small ΔΔG is within entropy-modulation range. Mode fully correct, ΔG ranking modestly off -> high but not full credit.
